projectScope.activities: The proposed project consists of excavation and reprocessing of approximately 6 million cubic metres of tailings to recover the iron-bearing mineral magnetite from the former Whitehorse Copper mine site. The proposal includes placement of processed tailings into the existing open pit and adjacent subsidence areas as well as back to the 'Old Pond' area. The proposed reclamation includes permanently removing tailings from the 'A' and 'B' Valleys to allow natural revegetation. Tailings are proposed to be returned to the 'Old Pond' area with the objective to create areas for industrial use. Approximately 23,000 m3/day of water is expected to be required for processing. Of this amount, the project expects approximately 220 m3/day of make-up water will be required after recycling the process water. Prior to use in processing pre-treatment of pit lake water is proposed via in-situ bioremediation. The project is located approximately 13 km southwest of downtown Whitehorse within the municipal boundaries. Access to the project area is by Mt. Sima Road. The activities are proposed to occur annually for up to eight months per year beginning in 2012. Project activities are expected to occur for approximately seven years and are considered to occur between March and November.

projectScope.activities: The proposed project entails deployment and operation of a hydro-kinetic (HKE) turbine in the Takhini River to generate power for pumping of water to irrigate agricultural land. The purpose of the project is to evaluate the performance and efficiency of HKE systems in Yukon river reaches and to assess the impacts of HKE devices on river environments. The proponent is applying for a Water Use Licence for generation of hydro power and has applied for Canadian Agricultural Adaptation Program (CAAP) funding. The device will be deployed in the Takhini River, near the Circle D Ranch property near km 1459 of the Alaska Highway, at one of two existing water withdrawal locations used by Circle D Ranch for agricultural irrigation. Power generated will be used to irrigate the existing Circle D Ranch operation subject to its own Water Use Licence (No. AG86-003). The project will occur over a series of two seasons, in the summers of 2012 and 2013, and will involve deployment of the HKE device in the first season for two to three periods lasting three to seven days each. Deployment in 2013 is proposed for July to September.

projectScope.activities: The proposed project involves clearing of land for the purpose of hay production and cattle grazing. The proponent has applied to the Yukon Government to extend their existing lot (Lot 1346) by an additional 9.5 ha of agricultural land. The project is located approximately 11 km from Whitehorse and is situated within one kilometre of the Takhini River. Project access will be by the Hotsprings Road and by an access route that runs through the proponent's existing parcel of land, north of the applied for area. Agricultural development will commence in the spring of 2011 and be complete by the summer of 2016. The proposed project has an indefinite lifespan.

projectScope.activities: The proposed project involves clearing of land and moving of earth for the purpose of replacing the FH Collins Secondary School located off Lewes Boulevard in Riverdale in the City of Whitehorse. Demolition of the existing school and construction of the new school comprise the project. The new school will be constructed on property overlapping with and directly adjacent to the existing building and will include the existing industrial arts wing. Gymnasium demolition will overlap with school operation. The existing school will continue to operate until the new school is ready for occupancy, after which time demolition of the old school will be completed. Some site infrastructure and access reconfiguration is scheduled to take place in the fall of 2011. Demolition is expected to commence in the spring of 2012 with the gymnasium and will continue to completion in the summer of 2014, after the new school is occupied. The new school is expected to be complete by August 2013.

projectScope.activities: The proposed project involves the underground development and mining of Lucky Queen and Onek deposits, and milling the ores at the existing Keno District Mill. The project entails silver/lead/zinc mining at two separate deposits found within the Keno Hill Silver District, and using the existing mine facilities of the Bellekeno mine in central Yukon. Alexco Keno Hill Mining Corp (AKHM) operates the existing Bellekeno Mine, which is located approximately 50km northeast of Mayo.Lucky Queen and Onek deposits are respectively, 4km and 500m northeast of Keno City. No change in the currently licensed and authorized tonnage for ore, waste or tailings deposition is being proposed. An amendment to the Type A Water Use Licence and Quartz Mining Licence will be required to support development of these two new deposits.

The principle activity is the continued operation of 7 diesel generators located at the site of the Whitehorse Rapids Power Development adjacent to the Whitehorse Rapids Dam in Whitehorse. The operation of the diesel generators occurs to supplement electricity to the Whitehorse Aishihik Faro (WAF) Grid to meet peak demands, to meet demands as a result of emergency conditions and as a result of routine maintenance. projectScope.activities: The proponent is proposing construction, operation, closure and reclamation of an open pit gold mine in central Yukon. The Eagle Gold Mine will produce 92 million tons of ore and 132 million tons of waste rock over a 9.2-year active mining phase, plus an additional year of gold recovery. The open pit mine will be developed using drill and blast technology. Gold extraction will utilize sodium cyanide heap leach technology.
projectScope.summary: Victoria Gold Corp is proposing the Eagle Gold Mine Project involving the design, construction, operation, closure, and reclamation of a gold mine in central Yukon. The proposed Project is located in central Yukon in the Traditional Territory of the First Nation of Na-Cho Nyak Dun, approximately 350 km north of Whitehorse, and 45 km (85 km by existing access roads) north-northeast of the Village of Mayo. The majority of the project site lies within the Dublin Gulch watershed and is proposed to be mined over ten years starting in 2014. The proposed project is comprised of an open pit mine and associated mine features, improvements to the Haggart Creek Road, and a 44 kilometre, 69 kV transmission line. The project is planned to have a twenty month construction phase, a nine year active mining phase, a ten year closure and reclamation phase, and a post-closure monitoring phase. The project will involve open pit mining at a production rate of approximately 29,500 tonnes per day. Gold extraction will utilize sodium cyanide heap leaching technology.The proposed project is located within the Dublin Gulch watershed approximately 350 km north of Whitehorse and 45 km northeast of the Village of Mayo. Access to the mine site is approximately 85 km from Mayo via existing access along the Silver Trail, South McQuesten Road, and Haggart Creek Road.
